title: Today's AI Brief: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab
summary: Exploring AI prototypes from Google's Futures Lab and an AI startup cleaning homes for free
imageAlt: Image from source article for Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.
imageCaption: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.
imageSourceTitle: Check out real-life AI prototypes from the Futures Lab.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm excited to share some fresh AI news with you. Today, we're looking at real-life AI prototypes that are making a difference. From learning Japanese through custom stories to practicing sign language with AI tutors, these innovations are worth checking out. It's amazing to see how AI can be used to make learning more accessible and fun.

First up, Google's Futures Lab has been working on some impressive AI prototypes. Students funded by Google have developed tools that use AI to generate custom stories for learning Japanese or provide real-time feedback on sign language practice. I've been digging into these projects, and it's cool to see the creative ways AI can be applied to education.

Next, an AI startup called Shift is taking a unique approach to training future robots. They're offering free home cleaning services in exchange for data to train their robots. The 'magic hat' they use will record their cleaners at work, providing valuable insights for AI development. I have to admit, I'm curious about how this will work out – a clean home for a dash of AI training data, it's an interesting trade-off.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
